head.hi: The Joint Chiefs of Staff to the Commander in Chief, Far East (Clark)

list.item:
We are aware that you are confronted with serious United States and internatl pub opinion problem at present and for future. We believe you will be assisted if we can work out means of giving improved support to your cmd through cooperation of appropriate agencies here to supply you with useful info and suggestions. This subj has been discussed in detail by appropriate ofcrs of Def, JCS, three mil services, State and CIA. Submitted herewith for your comment is analysis of
principal difficulties we have to meet together with suggestions for action here and by your cmd.

Communist propaganda is engaged in world-wide “hate America” campaign of an intensity, ferocity and vulgarity which is comparable to that directed at Germany at height of last war. Until recently allegations our use BW was principal theme. These charges continue but even greater accent is now being placed on charge of atrocities against POWs. Simultaneously, understandable doubts have been raised in United States and among our Allies by incidents on Koje and progress of talks at Panmunjom has created pessimism. Hence Communist anti-American campaign falls on receptive ground. Although Communist propaganda has not yet seized on ROK polit situation this too may be perverted to discredit United States unless we are alert.

We appreciate that series of circumstances, such as lull at battlefront, lack of news from protracted armistice negots are responsible for major attention to POW camp disorders and to some extent for resulting imbalance and lack of perspective in news coverage.

First suggestion is to arrange rapid and consistent flow info and advice on propaganda matters between us and elms your cmd which can make use of such material. We are prepared to set up here an interdepartmental watch committee to supply your needs. This committee would supply you with the daily résumé of United States news stories and editorial reaction to subjs concerning you. This would be accompanied when appropriate by suggestions for steps to be taken to correct unfavorable reactions. At frequent intervals it also would supply info on foreign pub opinion and on important Communist propaganda activities knowledge of which may not be readily available to you. You would see that appropriate ofcrs ur cmd receive and use this material and you would advise us on pub statements or other actions which might be taken here to reinforce ur posn.

In addn we are prepared to send you immediately two ranking specialists in PsyWar, one from State and other from Def in order acquaint you fully with special domestic and internatl problems which confront us. They would be available if you wish for limited time in order to assist in setting up coordinated mechanism which functions smoothly.

To illustrate reasoning which leads to above suggestion we cite these examples where exchange of info might have avoided difficulties or helped overcome them. Specifically our hope would be to supply info not readily available to you and which requires special interdepartmental effort here.
A.
We feel ur CX 69109In this telegram, dated May 26, 1952, CINCFE expressed its concern that the kinds of speculative and sensational news stories emanating from correspondents on the Kojedo and earlier incidents should be countered by public disclosure of a full narrative of important incidents which occurred at Koje-do prior to the Dodd case. CINCFE believed that the appearance of these stories with the implication of official secrecy was providing “the Communist propaganda machine” with ammunition (FE files, lot 55 D 128, tab 100). CSUSA, in telegram DA 909857, May 27, authorized CINCFE to prepare a complete but concise factual statement for clearance in Washington. (JCS files) is acute and accurate description ur problem with press on Koje. Censorship and other handling these correspondents is matter for your decision. You should know, however, that Canadian Emb has asked State if it would not be possible to remove correspondents or to exert stricter censorship and has objected to quotations attributed to three Canadian EM on Koje in Associated Press disp dtd 25 May. United States Emb London continues to report that British opinion is disturbed by evidence of “incompetence” in handling POWs and that officials do not feel we are doing enough to remove this impression. United States Consulate Hong Kong reports that Communist propaganda on POW issue is proving so effective that it recommends tight censorship from Koje. Publication of documentsNot printed (FE files, lot 55 D 128, tab 11). submitted yesterday to Senate Armed Services Cmte should help to correct this impression. Statements such as yours published in New York Times 3 June and determination shown by you and Gen BoatnerBrig. Gen. Haydon L. Boatner, USA, was appointed Commandant, Prisoner of War Camp, Koje-do, in May 1952 succeeding Brigadier General Dodd. also are corrective. Nevertheless, POW problem will confront our news and propaganda for some time. B.
At present United States and World Press features number seemingly true on spot reports, inadvertent comments by unidentified armed forces personnel. At same time it appears evident that Commies making determined effort to counter each of Gen Boatner’s strong moves with some sort of incident that provides copy for press, namely, tanks used to knock down flags which Commies re-erect next day and provide cause celebrate for press treatment. C.
Monitoring analysis all Soviet external and internal radio broadcasts for week ending 31 May show 1 percent devoted to BW and 16 percent to Korea. Of this 15 percent 14 was on POW issue.
